name: Pic de l'Àliga and Ermita de Santa Paulina Loop
name_original: Los valles de Ascó – circular desde Ascó por la Punta de l'Àgila
description: This difficult 22.6-mile mountain biking route from Ascó offers panoramic views from Punta de l'Àgila and varied terrain.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Ascó Spain offer a diverse landscape for riders, situated in the Ribera d'Ebre comarca of Catalonia. The region features a mix of flatlands, rolling hills, and mountainous terrain, including the Serra de la Fatarella to the east. The Ebro River flows through the area, providing riverside paths and contributing to the varied topography. This environment supports a range of mountain biking experiences, from gentle routes along the river to more challenging ascents in the hills. Best mountain bike trails around Ascó The most popular mountain bike trail is GR 99: Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre Loop, a 26.1 miles (42.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This route follows sections of the GR-99, offering views along the Ebro River. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pic de l'Àliga and Ermita de Santa Paulina Loop, a difficult 22.6 miles (36.4 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, leading to viewpoints like Pic de l'Àliga and Ermita de Santa Paulina. Local mountain bikers also love the Riba-roja d'Ebre – Sebes Nature Reserve Boardwalk loop from Flix, a 12.9 miles (20.8 km) trail leading through riverside landscapes and the Sebes Nature Reserve, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Mountain biking around Ascó is defined by the Ebro River, the varied terrain of the Serra de la Fatarella, and converted railway lines known as Vias Verdes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside rides to more demanding mountain ascents. The routes in Ascó are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ascó's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Ascó?
A: Ascó offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 130 routes available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ascó?
A: The terrain around Ascó is highly diverse. You'll find everything from flatlands and rolling hills to mountainous areas like the Serra de la Fatarella. The Ebro River also provides opportunities for leisurely cycling along its banks, often on dedicated paths. Expect a mix of natural surfaces, forest trails, and sections of converted railway lines (Vias Verdes).

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Ascó?
A: Ascó's mountain bike trails offer a range of difficulties. There are 24 easy routes, 93 moderate routes, and 13 difficult routes. This variety ensures options for beginners looking for gentle rides and experienced riders seeking more challenging climbs and technical descents.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Ascó?
A: Ascó enjoys a Mediterranean climate, with hot, dry summers and mild winters. This makes it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding, while winter can also be enjoyable due to the mild conditions. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Ascó?
A: Yes, Ascó has family-friendly options, particularly along the Ebro River and on sections of the Vias Verdes (Greenways). These converted railway lines offer gentle gradients and scenic routes, making them accessible for riders of all ages and skill levels. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38243491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riba-roja d'Ebre – Sebes Nature Reserve Boardwalk loop from Flix&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that takes you through riverside landscapes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on mountain bike trails in Ascó?
A: Many natural trails in the Ascó region are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept under control. However, it's always best to check specific local regulations or signage for individual routes or protected areas. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the ride and bring plenty of water for both of you.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see while mountain biking around Ascó?
A: The region around Ascó is rich in history and natural beauty. You can encounter various landmarks, including historic castles and charming villages. Notable attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5134983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Móra d’Ebre&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5122996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flix Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5129643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet Ferry Crossing&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4200227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Nativity of the Virgin Mary in Miravet&lt;/a&gt;, offering cultural points of interest alongside your ride.

Q: Are there any long-distance cycling paths or greenways in the area?
A: Yes, Ascó is connected to Spain's Vias Verdes network, which are old railway lines converted into cycling and walking paths, known for their gentle gradients. Additionally, the GR-99 (Ebro Nature Trail) follows the Ebro River, offering extensive long-distance cycling opportunities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944315935/gr-99-flix-and-riba-roja-d-ebre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 99: Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route utilizing sections of this trail.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Ascó?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views along the Ebro River, and the challenging ascents in the hills. The diverse landscape caters to different preferences, from tranquil riverside rides to more demanding mountain adventures.

Q: Are there many circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Ascó are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. This is convenient for riders who drive to the trailheads. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944315935/gr-99-flix-and-riba-roja-d-ebre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 99: Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945427488/pic-de-l-aliga-and-ermita-de-santa-paulina-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de l'Àliga and Ermita de Santa Paulina Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available near mountain bike trailheads in Ascó?
A: While specific parking information varies by trailhead, Ascó and surrounding towns generally offer public parking options. Many routes start from accessible points within or near villages, where parking is typically available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for the most convenient parking locations.

Q: Do I need a permit for mountain biking in Ascó?
A: Generally, you do not need a specific permit for mountain biking on public trails in the Ascó region. However, always respect private property, follow local signage, and adhere to any regulations within protected natural areas. If you plan to ride through specific nature reserves, it's wise to check for any particular rules they might have.
tips: Beautiful hermitage on the outskirts of Ascó with a good picnic area, right next to the hermitage.
Peak located in the Sierra del Tormo, between Ascó and Garcia, with spectacular views of the Ribera del Ebro.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Los valles de Ascó mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route is quite demanding. It typically takes around 3 hours and 37 minutes to complete, but this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as difficult. It's considered a 'Hard mountain bike ride' requiring 'Very good fitness' and 'Advanced riding skills' due to its length, significant elevation gain, and varied terrain.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is not recommended for beginners. Its 'difficult' rating, combined with the need for 'Very good fitness' and 'Advanced riding skills', means it's best suited for experienced mountain bikers.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail starting and ending in Ascó. Specific parking information for this exact trailhead isn't detailed, but you can typically find public parking within the town of Ascó. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a highly varied and often technical terrain. The route features a mix of unpaved, paved, natural, asphalt, and gravel sections. Some parts can be very tortuous, with many stones and large potholes, potentially requiring advanced handling skills.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the Ermita de Santa Paulina and the Pic de l'Àliga. The summit of Punta de l'Àgila (Pic de l'Àliga) is particularly notable for its spectacular panoramic views over the Ribera d'Ebre and its valley.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: While specific recommendations for this route are not available, mountain biking in the Ascó area is generally well-regarded. Spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for challenging rides like this, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential winter conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Los valles de Ascó route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for this specific mountain bike route is not explicitly stated. However, when mountain biking with dogs, it's generally recommended to ensure they are well-trained, on a leash, and capable of handling the route's difficulty and length. Always carry enough water for both yourself and your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access this mountain bike route. It is generally open to the public.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will encounter sections of the Camí de la Vall de Caete, Pista de Vall de Cigales, Camí de l'Era del Pentinat, and Camí del Mas de Prades, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and appropriate protective gear. A GPS device with the route loaded is also highly recommended due to the varied terrain and intersecting paths.
